paper_title: High-resolution oxygen isotope record and construction of the age-depth model

methods.description: This method involves aligning a new planktonic δ 18 O depth series with existing datasets and astronomical models to construct an age-depth model. The alignment is done to ensure continuity and consistency in the isotopic data, which is crucial for understanding past climate conditions.

steps.description: Obtain the new planktonic δ 18 O depth series from 55.65 to 82.90 mcd.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch new planktonic δ 18 O depth series data
outputs: new planktonic δ 18 O depth series

steps.description: Connect the new dataset to the previously published dataset (83.4-106.41 mcd).
steps.searchable_summary: connect new dataset to existing dataset

steps.description: Align the planktonic δ 18 O record with a Southern Hemisphere eccentricity-tilt-precession (ETP) composite and the LR04 benthic stack.
steps.searchable_summary: align δ 18 O record with ETP composite and LR04 stack

steps.description: Construct the age-depth model using the aligned isotope record and target curves with two tie points.
steps.searchable_summary: construct age-depth model with tie points

steps.description: Minimize circular reasoning by limiting alignment to two tie points and using a biostratigraphic tie point at 1.93 Ma.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model by minimizing circular reasoning
